def: s.f. = H بهنسار भनसार bhansār, s.m. Fire-place, &c. ( = bhaṛsāʼīṅ; bhāṛ).
v.n. = bhasnā, q.v. v.n. To be manifest, to appear, be known.
s.m. The medicinal plant Clerodeṅdron infortunatum (Linn.), or Volkameria infortunata (Roxb.).
more: s. m. A medicinal plant. (Clerodendron infortunatum: Lin. Volkameria infortunata: Roxb. ).

def: s.m. = bhāṭā; bhaṅṭā, qq.v. s.m. = bhattā, q.v. s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest; ploughman's wages in kind; additional allowance, extra allowance or pay to public servants on special duty, or to officers and soldiers (commonly called 'batta'); subsistence money; travelling allowance
more: s. m. The egg plant. (Solanum melongena).

def: s.m. The egg-plant, Solanum melongena (syn. baiṅgan).
s.m. Ploughman's wages in kind (see bhāt, bhātā, bhattā).

s.f. Way, manner, mode, method, style, fashion; variety, kind, sort, class; — bhāṅṭ-bhāṅṭ or bhāṅti-bhāṅti (-ke), adj. Various kinds (of), various, diverse, of many kinds or sorts, miscellaneous, multifarious
more: n. f. S. भिद् to divide. 1. kind; sort; division; class; order. Bolī hai bahū bhāṅt kī jo koī bole jān, Hīye tarāzū tol-ke, mukh se bāhar ān . Kabīr. Speech is of various kinds, as doth a master only know, Weigh well your words in your own mind before you let them flow. 2. Manner; mode; way; method. ( ḍhang ). bhāṅt bhāṅt kā , adj. Various; of many sorts; diverse; miscellaneous; multifarious; promiscuous; motley.
n. m. S. भण्डिका A medicinal plant; Volkameria infortunata . Wat.
s. f. Manner, mode, method, kind, sort. بهانت بهانت bhānt-bhānt or bhānti-bhānti, adj. Various.
subs: bhalī-bhāṅt, adv. Well, thoroughly, fully, quite, soundly, severely, &c.
kisī bhāṅti adv. By some means

def: s.m. The fruit of the koʼī or water-lily.
s.m. = biṭmās, q.v. s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
n. m. ( Slang ). 1. Thumb. 2. The male organ.
s.m. The fruit of the koʼī or water-lily.
more: s. m. A sort of pulse.
s. m. Name of a grain, the fruit of the کوٴي koʼī or water lilly.

def: s.m. = biṭmās, q.v. s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
s.m. = بهان bhānu, q.v. s.m. Appearance, brightness, light; ray of light; the sun; a planet; any luminary; master; sovereign
more: s. m. The sun, a ray of light: see بهان bhānu.
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. bhānu-matī), Luminous, splendid, resplendent; beautiful, handsome; — the sun; a handsome person
s.f. A beautiful or handsome woman

def: s.m. Appearance, brightness, light; ray of light; the sun; a planet; any luminary; master; sovereign
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. bhānu-matī), Luminous, splendid, resplendent; beautiful, handsome; — the sun; a handsome person
s.f. A beautiful or handsome woman

def: s.f. See s.v. bhānu. s.m. Appearance, brightness, light; ray of light; the sun; a planet; any luminary; master; sovereign
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. bhānu-matī), Luminous, splendid, resplendent; beautiful, handsome; — the sun; a handsome person
s.f. A beautiful or handsome woman

def: s.m. Name of a small class of Rājpūts.
s. f. Eyebrow or eyebrows. See بهون bhauṅ.
s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
more: n. f. H. bhirmnā to wander. Circumambulation of the bride and bridegroom round the sacred fire. bhāṅvar phirnā, yā paṛnā , v. n. To circumambulate.
s. f. Revolution, circulation. بهانورپاڙناياپهرنا bhāṅwar pāṛnā or phirnā, or بهانوري پهرنا bhāṅwrī-phirnā, v. n. 1. To circle, to circumambulate. 2. To be sacrificed.
subs: bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.m. Revolution, gyration, whirl; whirlpool, eddy, vortex (syn. gird-āb); a large black bee, the humble bee; creeping plant, creeper
more: n. f. S. भ्रम to whirl round. Revolution; gyration; eddy; whirlpool. bhaṅwar paṛnā , v. n. To be formed (an eddy). bhaṅwar jāl , n. m. ( Met .) The world and its snares. bhaṅwar meṅ paṛnā, yā phirnā , v. n. To whirl round; gyrate.
s. m. A whirlpool, eddy, vortex.
s. m. 1. A large black bee. 2. A climbing plant, creeper.
subs: s.m. (Met.), The world and its snares
(S. bhramara+kaṭa+ikā), s.f. A turning or movable ring attached to a rope (for confining an animal or thing); collar or halter (for a dog, goat, &c.); a swivel
s.m.f. The songs which the cowherdesses of Mathurā addressed to the bees
v.n. To whirl round, revolve; to get into a whirl, &c.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھنور (bhnvr /⁠bhãvar⁠/) (c. 1658), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀪𑀫𑀭 (bhamara), from Sanskrit भ्रमर (bhramará, “large black bee; spinning top”).

def: n. f. A kind of halter or chain (for horses, dogs, etc.); a swivel.
more: s. f. A collar or halter (for a dog, goat, &c.). See بهونرکلي.

def: v.n. To go round, &c. = bhāṅwar phirnā, q.v.s.v. bhāṅwar. s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
subs: bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.f. = bhaṅbīrī; bhaṅwrī, qq.v. v.n. To run swiftly (said of a child).

s.m. A large black bee, &c. ( = bhaṅwar and bhauṅrā, qq.v.). s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
more: s. m. 1. A large black bee. 2. A climbing plant, creeper.
subs: bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.f. = bhāṅwar, q.v.
more: s. f. Revolution, circulation. بهانورپاڙناياپهرنا bhāṅwar pāṛnā or phirnā, or بهانوري پهرنا bhāṅwrī-phirnā, v. n. 1. To circle, to circumambulate. 2. To be sacrificed.
subs: v.n. = bhāṅwar phirnā, q.v. s.f. Going round, turning round; revolution, circulation, circumambulation; — marriage (since an important ceremony at Hindū marriages consists in going seven times round the sacred fire)
bhāṅwar phirnā (-ki), To go round; to circle, circumambulate; to be married; to be sacrificed.

def: s.f. dim. of bhaṅwrā, q.v.
s.f. Rent paid to a zamīndār in kind (usually to the extent of half the produce); distribution of the crop between the landlord and the cultivator
subs: s.m. Land that has long been cultivated
s.m. Land recently brought under cultivation.

def: postpos. See بهاوين bhāweṅ.

postp. = bhāveṅ, q.v. = S بهاوي भावे bhāwe, postp. In the sentiment, perception, mind, notion or idea (of, -ke); in consideration (of); with regard (to); concerning, for; with a view (to), for the purpose (of), in order that; in the manner (of), after the fashion (of), like (as); in the eyes (of), near, before, to
v.n. To revolve, to whirl ( = bhamnā; bhaunā).
v.n. = bhānā, q.v. v.n. To be approved (of), to be acceptable (to, -ko), be pleasing (to), to please; to be beloved, be held dear; to suit, fit, become; to seem good or befitting (syn. aććhā lagnā).
adj. = bhāvī, q.v. s.m. Future time, the future
subs: adj. Dependent on, or subject to, the future.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Amiable, dear, loved; — beloved, love.
s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; hot breath ( = bāph)
more: E. n. m. S. वाष्प, Pr. vappho . Steam; vapour; exhalation. bhāp bharānā , v. a. To blow the secretion (popularly the breath) from the mother's crop into the crop of the young pigeon. bhāp nikalnā , v. n. To emit steam or vapour.
subs: v.t. To feed its young with its bill (a pigeon).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi بھاپھہ (bhaphh /⁠bhāph⁠/) (c. 1419) and later as بھاپ (bhap /⁠bhāp⁠/), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀩𑀧𑁆𑀨 (bappha), from Sanskrit वाष्प (vāṣpa, “tear”).

def: v.n. = bhabaknā, q.v. v.n. To be heated; to simmer, boil, bubble; to give out steam; to fume; to catch fire, to burn, be burnt or scalded; to burst into flame, blaze up suddenly; to be enraged or exasperated, fly into a passion, to rush on with rage; to run with great speed (a horse); to roar (as a tiger); to be poured out, gush forth: — bhabak-jānā = bhabaknā.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To break out (as, a fire: Harris ).

def: s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
more: Old. H. n. m. H. bhāp steam. A vapour-bath (E. unhā ).
n. m. Deception; deceit; fraud. Ajī, apnā kām karo, kis lapāṭye ke bhapāṛe meṅ āë .

def: s.m. Breath; wheedling, deception, deceit, fraud (syn. dam).
s. f. Steam, vapour, exhalation. See باپهـ bāph.
s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; hot breath ( = bāph)
subs: v.t. To feed its young with its bill (a pigeon).

def: s.m. = bhapārā, q.v. s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
v.t. To conceive; to guess; to comprehend; to make out, know, see through.
more: v. a. To conceive, to guess, to comprehend. See H. تاڙنا tāṛnā.

def: s.m. = bhabkā, q.v. s.m. Blast from a furnace; alembic, still; — a kind of drinking vessel with a large mouth.
s.m. = bhapārā, q.v. s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
more: s. m. (dakh.) Exhalation, steam, fumigation ( Harris ): see بهپهارا bhaphārā.

def: v.t. To conceive; to guess; to comprehend; to make out, know, see through.
s.m. = phapolā, q.v. s.m. A blister, vesicle; a bubble
subs: v.n. To be blistered; (met.) to be afflicted in mind
v.n. To satisfy a revenge that has long been rankling in one's breast; to give vent to one's passion, appease one's wrath.

def: s.m. = bhapāṛā, q.v. s.m. Steam, vapour, exhalation; fumigation, fermentation; vapour bath.
s.m. Load, burden ( = bojh); weight, gravity; a weight of gold equal to 2,000 palas; fagot; yoke for carrying burdens; charge, trust, responsibility
subs: (-kā), To put down a load or burden; to throw off the responsibility (of); to deliver (from), to redeem, make atonement (for)
s.m. A cart ( = P. bār-kash); the strap which fastens a horse, &c. to the shafts or pole of a carriage, or cart, &c.
adj. & s.m. Weighted, loaded, burdened, oppressed; — an oppressed person, &c.
bhār-vāhik, s.m. Bearer of burdens, porter, carrier (syn. moṭiyā; kahār)
(vulg. bhār-br̤iksh), s.m. A fragrant substance (commonly called kākshī), considered variously as a vegetable and a mineral product.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَهَار (bahār, “spring”). Doublet of وَسَنْت (vasant) and بَسَنْت (basant).

def: adj. Corr. of bāhir or bāhar, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بَهَار (bahār, “spring”). Doublet of وَسَنْت (vasant) and بَسَنْت (basant).

def: s.m. Oven, furnace, kiln, fire-place for parching grain
more: s. m. A furnace, kiln (particularly, for parching grain).
s. m. The price of fornication. بهاڙکهانا bhāṛ khānā, v. a. To live on the wages of prostitution (not the woman, but her dependants).
subs: v.n. To light or heat the oven; to make a fire of sticks, leaves, &c. for parching grain, &c.; to put fuel on the fire, feed the fire; (fig.) to take up a mean occupation
bhāṛ-meṅ ḍālnā, v.t. To throw into the fire; to fling away, cast to the dogs.

def: s.m. Wages of prostitution; price of fornication
subs: v.n. To live on the wages of prostitution (not the woman, but her dependants); — s.m. A pimp, &c. ( = bhaṛuʼā, q.v.).

def: s.m. Load, burden; plenty, size, bulk
more: adj. S. भृ to fill. Used in comp. Full; all; whole; up to; as much as; as far as. Ser bhar . One seer. Umar bhar . During one's whole life.

n. m. 1. The sound made by the emission of wind from behind. 2. Drumming; rat-a-tat; rub-a-dub. 3. The crackling of burning wood; the report of musketry. bhaṛ bhaṛ karnā , v. a. To make a noise like a drum. 2. To crack; crackle.

adj. Full, as much as, as far as, up to, whole, all, bulk, size, every, each. As, بهرمقدور bhar maḳdūr, or مقدوربهر maḳdūr bhar, To the best of oneʼs power, with all oneʼs might; بانس بهر bāns bhar, The height or length of a bamboo; سيربهر ser bhar, A ser; عمربهر uﻌmr bhar, All lifetime. کوس بهر kos bhar, A kos; whereas بهرکوس bhar kos would mean a full kos.
subs: v.n. To be paid in full, to receive the amount due; to be paid off, get one's deserts
s.f. Receipt in full (the name being derived from the use of these words in the body of the receipt)
bhar-pūraṇ, adj. Quite full, brimful, chokeful, crammed, replete, overflowing; sufficient, quite enough; high (as a tide); full (as the moon)
s.f. The state of being full or overflowing, fullness; abundance
adv. In full of all demands
adv. At full gallop; — to the extent of one's power, &c. = bhar-śak
bhar-śak, adv. To the extent of one's ability, as far as possible, as far as may be; with all one's might
s.m. A full kos; fully a kos
adv. Such a long time, all that while
adv. About a ser; a ser
adv. About a kos; a kos (cf. bhar-kos)

def: s.m. Name of one of the aboriginal races of India
subs: s.m. A subdivision of the bhar tribe.

def: s.m. Large boat, barge, lighter.
more: s. m. A large boat, a lighter.

def: s.m. Sound of crackling fuel, crackle; crash; roll (of musketry); rat-tat; rush, whir; sound of breaking wind; — bhaṛ-bhaṛ, s.m. = bhaṛ (and is the more common form); adv. With a crash, or gush or rush, &c.
subs: v.n. To burn fiercely; to roar, crackle, crack, crash, &c.

def: n. m. S. भृ to bear. A burden. بوحهـ bhār utār'nā , v. n. See bojh utārnā . To redeem; deliver from; make atonement. Pirthivī kā bhār utārne ko autār dhāran kīyā .
more: n. m. S. भाष्ट्र from भस्ज् to cook. A fire place for parching grain; a large oven. Sūrat na shakal, bhāṛ se nikal . Prov. Nor face nor figure, out of the oven sure. bhāṛ jhoknā , v. n. 1. To make a fire of sticks, leaves, etc. for parching gram, rice, etc; to supply fuel for the fire; to feed the fire. 2. To take up any mean occupation. Bārah baras Dillī meṅ rahe bhāṛ hī jhokā . Prov. Twelve years in Delhī and only a fireman (for parching grain)! (Well bred, to evil wed). bhāṛ meṅ jhok'nā, yā ḍālnā, chūlhe meṅ ḍālnā , v. a. lit . To throw into the fire. To cast to the dogs; throw before swine. Mo ko, na to ko, le bhāṛ meṅ jhoko . Rus. Prov. Not for me nor thee, into the fire it goes.

n. m. ( Slang ). S. भाटक hire, from भट् to support. The wages of a prostitute. bhāṛ khānā , v. n. To live on the earnings of a prostitute. bhā'ṛū, bhāṛ-khānā , n. m. 1. A pimp ( bhaṛwā ); a cuckold. 2. A fool; blockhead.
s. m. A load or burthen, a great weight of gold, weight, gravity, fagot. (Pers. بار). ¶ bhār (dakh.) See باهر.
adv. (dakh.) Without, &c.: see باهر bāhar: see, also, P. بہار bahār.

def: v. a. 1. To pay. 2. To fill. 3. To reimburse. 4. To darn.

v. n. 1. To be paid in full; to be satisfied. 2. To be discharged, or liquidated (a debt). 3. To get one's deserts; to receive one's reward. 1. Jaisā kīyā thā, waisā bhar pāyā . 2. Jām-i-khālī se jo sāqī ne mujhe ḍahkāyā, Maiṅ kahā, 'bakhshye, sāhab, mujhe, maiṅ bhar payā .' Saudā. The goblet-boy enticed me with an empty cup, I said, 'your pardon, Sir, I've had my fill of sup.'
more: v. a. 1. To be paid, to receive the full amount. 2. ( Met. ) It is used when a person is disappointed; as, مين ني بهرپايا maiṅ ne bhar pāyā, I am paid.

def: s.m. Load, burden ( = bhār); throwing any burden or responsibility on a person; — scaffolding.
more: n. m. Hire; carriage; freight; fare; rent (of a house). Shekhī seṭh kī, dhotī bhāṛe kī! Prov. The pride of a Seṭh (a rich banker), and a borrowed dhotī (covering for the loins)! bhāṛe kā ṭaṭṭū , n. m. lit . A hired pony; hackney. The slave of necessity or habit.

def: s.m. Hire, fare, freight, carriage, rent; price, recompense
subs: s.m. Hired pony; hack; (fig.) the slave of necessity or habit.

def: s.m. A kind of grass ( = bhaḍā: cf. bharī).

adj. 1. Thriving; prosperous; abounding; wealthy; fortunate; happy. Phr. One's star being in the ascendant. 2. Abounding in wealth and children; possessing a happy family. Dātā tumheṅ bharā purā rakkhe ! Benediction. May God the giver endow you freely of his abundance!
part. adj. Filled, filled with, full, full of, replete, brimful, overflowing (com. used at the end of comp., e.g. mal-bharā, adj. Replete with filth
more: p. p. of bharnā , q. v.
adj. Full, replete. بهراپرا bharā purā, Filled, full.
subs: adj. Charged with venom); — s.m. Filing; fulness; contents, load, cargo, charge (of a gun)
adj. Wealthy, prosperous, flourishing, thriving, fortunate, happy; having in abundance; abounding in; having a large family.

def: s.m. A skylark; wild cotton; a bone; — a proper name.
s.m. lit. 'Bringing or bearing food'; the skylark (com. called bhart).
s.f. Filling, stuffing ( = bharāwaṭ); — price paid for filling or stuffing.
more: n. f. 1. The act of filling or stuffing. 2. Watering (a field); irrigation. 3. ( bharvāī ) The price paid for water, padding, etc. bharāī, yā bharāo karnā , v. a.; To fill; stuff; water.

def: s.f. A cess or tax (in the Benares district), one half of which is given to the ʻāmil for charges of remittance, and the other carried to the credit of government.
s.m. = bhaṛait, q.v.
s.m. = bhaṛaitī, q.v. s.m. Tenant, lessee
s.m. Tenant, lessee
more: (from بهاڙا Hire) s. m. A tenant, a lessee.

def: n. m. A tenant (of a house). bhaṛaitī dar bhaṛaitī , n. m. A sub-tenant (of a house).
s.m. (local), Fruit-garden, orchard.
more: n. f. 1. Splendour; glare; blaze; flash. 1. Purānā ṭhīkrā, aur qalaī kī bhaṛak . Prov. An old brick silvered thick. (An old woman dressed out as a young person). 2. Bhaṛak bhārī, khīsā khālī . Prov. Great show, an empty pocket. 2. Ostentation; pomp; parade; pageantry. 3. Explosion; burst; flame. 4. Rage; fury; passion; paroxyism. 5. Startling; shying (in animals). Abhī nayā hai, is se bhaṛak hai . A wild young colt will shy and bolt. bhaṛak-dār, bhaṛkavval , Ped. fauq-ul-bhaṛak , adj. Splendid; blazing; shining; refulgent.

ety: S. स्फर = स्फुर+क; cf. also S. भर्ग
def: s.f. Splendour, glitter, flash, blaze; show, pomp, parade, pageantry; burst, explosion; flame; burst of anger, paroxysm, rage, fury; perturbation, agitation, alarm; starting; shying (of a horse); start, shy; quivering, throb, pulsation, twitch, &c.
more: (from بهڙکنا) s. f. 1. Splendour, blaze, flash, glare, show. 2. Perturbation, agitation, alarm, starting, shiness (in animals).
subs: adj. Splendid, shining, glittering, blazing, refulgent.

def: v.n. To start, shrink, be scared or alarmed; to shy (a horse); to throb violently, beat, palpitate, quiver, twitch (as the eyelid, &c.); to break or burst forth into flame, blaze up, break out; to take fire; to fly into a passion, become very excited; to be over-heated; to break, crack (as a vessel); to be dislocated, be displaced.
more: v. n. 1. To start, to shrink, to be scared, to be alarmed. 2. To be blown up into a flame, to blaze forth (fire).

ety: S. धर्म, by change of initial consonant; though supposed by some to come from S. भ्रम
def: s.m. Character, reputation, esteem, credit
more: n. m. S. भ्रम. 1. Wandering; deviation; aberration. 2. Perplexity; confusion; maze; labyrynth. 3. Misapprehension; error; blunder; mistake. Kaun bharam bhūle ho, man gyānī? 4. Presumption; supposition; suspicion; doubt. 5. Credit; character; repute; account. bharam bāṅdhnā , v. a. To establish one's credit. bharam k . v. a. 1. To suspect; doubt; question. 2. To believe; depend on; rely upon. 3. To be vain, proud, puffed up, vain-glorious. bharam khulnā , v. n. To be betrayed (a secret), exposed (pretensions). bharam gaṅvānā , v. a. To lose one's credit. Tulsī, par ghar jāë-ke dukkh na kahīye roë, Bharam gaṅvāve āpno, bāṅṭ na sakke koë . Tulsī Dās. Tulsī, weep not, your sorrow e'er among strange men, Your credit sunk in vain, no one to share your pain.
s. m. 1. (for भ्रम ) Errour, mistake, suspicion, apprehension, perplexity, doubt. 2. (for संभ्रम ) s. m. Credit, character, reputation. بهرم گنوانا bharam-gaṅwānā, v. a. To lose character.
subs: v.t. To gain a reputation; to establish (one's) credit
v.t. To credit, believe, rely upon, depend on
v.t. To sacrifice (one's) reputation or character, to lose (one's) credit.

def: n. m. H. bharam . 1. Secret ( bhed ); plot. 2. Credit; fame; character; good name. bhaṛambā kholnā , v. a. To divest of false appearances; expose; take off the mask.

s.m. The act of carrying, bearing, supporting, maintaining, sustaining, nourishing; filling, satisfying; paying in full; wages, hire; — bharan, s.f. Heavy shower of rain; — postp. To, up to, till ( = tak)
more: n. f. S. भृ to fill. A heavy shower of rain. Sāvan kī jhaṛī, Bhādoṅ kī bharan . Song.
1. adv. Up to, to, till. 2. ( भरण ) s. Filling, satisfying.
subs: s.m. Supply of food and raiment, maintenance.

def: v.t. To cause to be full; to cause to fill; to fill; to feed its young (a bird); to have (a mare) covered.
more: v. a. Caus. of بهرنا q. v. To feed its young out of the hen-bird's mouth. bharāo , n. m. Stuffing; padding; stuff.
v. a. 1. To fill, to cause to fill. 2. To cover (a mare).

def: adj. & s.m. Speaking from the impulse of the moment; blurting out without consideration whatever comes uppermost; simple, guileless, artless; plain-spoken, candid; — one who blurts out whatever comes uppermost, &c.; a simple, or guileless person, &c.; — prater, chatterbox, babbler ( = baṛ baṛiyā).
adj. 1. Babbling; gossiping; unable to keep a secret. Bhaṛbhaṛyā achchhā, peṭ kā kapṭī burā . Prov. Better a babbler than a close knave. 2. Open; candid; frank; without guile.
s.m. A caste of Hindūs whose occupation is to parch grain; a member of this caste, a grain-parcher.
more: s. m. A man who parches grain.

def: n. m. bhaṛ-bhūṅjan , n. f. S. भाषट्रभर्जक, H. bhāṛ oven, bhūnnā to fry. A grain-parcher, a caste employed in parching different sorts of grain, pulse, etc. 1. Baiṭhe bisātī rāh meṅ tinke se chunte haiṅ, Jalte haiṅ nān-bāī, to bhaṛbhūṅje bhunte hain . Shahr-āshob. A ruined town. Nazīr. Each pedlar idly sits and straws picks all the day, If bakers burn, the firemen roasted are midway. 2. Lālā Bhaṛbhūṅjā bhāṛ bhūn rahā hai. Bon mot at the exhibition of fireworks at the Imperial assemblage at Delhi. The Lala grain-parcher is surely frying up there.
s.f. Woman of the bhaṛbhūṅjā caste; wife of a bhaṛbhūṅjā; a woman who parches grain.
more: s. f. A woman who parches grain.

adj. Heavy, weighty, ponderous, massive, unwieldy; large, big, bulky; great, grand; crowded (as a meeting or fair); swollen, inflamed; of importance, important, momentous, grave, serious; valuable, costly; patient; difficult, hard, laborious, burdensome, troublesome, grievous, oppressive, unsupportable, insufferable, distressing; dull, dun, dark (as colour); heavy-footed, slow, sluggish (of pace); hoarse, deep, bass (voice); hard-of-hearing; loud-sounding; sad, dull, dejected (in mind or spirits), languid; — s.m. more: adj. S. भार, P. bār burden. 1. Heavy; weighty; ponderous; massive; unwieldy. 1. Bhārī patthar chūmā, aur chhoṛ dīyā . Prov. The heavy stone just touched and left alone. 2. Joṅ joṅ bhīje kāmlī, toṅ toṅ bhārī hoë . Prov. The more the woollen covering's wet, the heavier it grows. 3. Mūlī apne hī pattoṅ bhārī hai . Prov. The weight of its own leaves is too much for the radish. (He cannot support himself, and he has a crowd of relations hanging on him). 4. Koī moloṅ bhārī, koī toloṅ bhārī . Prov. Some worth have great, some only weight. 5. Sugandh lagāūṅ to ūbh marūṅ, ūbh marūṅ pahne tan sārī Hār chaṅbelī kā bhārī lagat, tum jānat ho tan kī sukhwārī! Scents make me faint, the weight of clothes would kill me quite, A jasmine wreath would weigh me down, you know I am so slight! 2. Solid; firm; stable. 3. Large; considerable; great; voluminous. Baṛī bhārī kitāb banāī . 4. Weighty; important; mom…

To be contained to fullness, be filled or filled up, be full; to be satisfied or sated; to be performed, be finished, be complete; to fill up, heal (as a wound); to be covered (as a mare), be lined (as a bitch); to be poured in; to abound, be replete (with, -se); to be loaded or laden, be charged (as a gun); to be stuffed, crammed, choked, stopped up (but in this sense bhar-jānā is the usual form); to be stored, stacked, heaped up, accumulated; to be watered or irrigated (as a field); to be steeped (in, -meṅ), soaked, drenched; to be painted, coloured, daubed, stained, polluted, defiled; to be cast (in a mould); to be paid in full (a debt), be refunded, be remitted; to be made good, be restored; to be full or ripe, come to a head, be full to bursting; to swell (as the feet, &c.); to develop, become plump or well-conditioned (the body); — v.t. To fill; to satisfy; to bestow in abundance, make rich; to perform, do, fulfil, complete, make good, discharge; to liquidate, pay, repay, re-imburse; to load, charge; to fill (colour, in -meṅ), to daub, paint, stain, defile, pollute; to soak or steep (in, -meṅ); to weave, plait, intertwine; to wind, twine; to give (evidence); to repeat (a creed, &c.); to pay (the penalty of), to undergo, suffer, bear, endure; to make (one) pay; to stir up, excite, instigate, incite
more: v. n. 1. To be full, filled up, replenished. 1. Phūl phūl kar-ke chaṅger bhartī hai . Prov. With gathering flowers and flowers the basket's full. 2. Bhar de, bhar pāve; Kāl kanṭak pās na āve . Mendicants' cry. Who filleth shall be filled, and the enemy Famine shall never come near him. 2. To be complete, finished, over. 3. To be stuffed, choked, filled, stopped. 4. To be charged, laden (a gun), fraught. 5. To be poured in, put in. 6. To be irrigated, watered (a field). 7. To be cast in a mould or matrix. 8. To be painted, colored (a picture). 9. To be steeped, soaked, drenched. Pāṅchoṅ kapṛe raṅg meṅ bhar gaë . 10. To overflow; to be flooded, inundated. Jal thal bhar gaë . 11. To be stored, stacked, accumulated, piled. 12. To be granulated (as a wound); to be healed. 13. To be filled up (a post). A sāmī bhar gaī . 14. To be full or ripe; to come to a head (a boil); (meṭ.) to be full to b…

To do; perform; execute; fulfil; discharge. 3. To weave; intertwine; plait; mat. 4. To reel; wind. Nalī bharnā . 5. To endow; bestow; make rich or prosperous. 1. Khudā bhare hī ko bhartā hai . Prov. Who knows what yesterday was done, and what the Lord will do to-day, Which one brought down, and which raised up, which robbed, and which made rich to-day? 6. To offer; make offerings. Chaukī bharnā . 7. To give evidence; attest; corroborate. 8. To take measured steps; to march; cover a piece of ground. Ḍig bharnā . 9. To take; exact; extort; make one p…

s. m. The name of a bird (a species of lark), a skylark. ¶ bhrit (or bhr̤itya for भृत्य ) s. m. A servant.

def: s.m. Cherisher, nourisher, supporter, protector; husband, lord
more: n. m. 1. Providence ( dātā ). God. 2. Husband; support; stay.
s. m. Vegetables boiled or fried and broken in the hand.

def: s.m. = bhatār, bhartā, qq.v. s.m. Husband

s.f. Filling, insertion, stuffing, &c.; cargo, lading, &c. ( = bharat, q.v.); store, stock, accumulation; filling in, completion; admission, enrolment, enlistment, recruiting (soldiers); promotion (a vulg. use); — additional and irrelevant matter, invention, fabrication, falsehood
more: n. f. Cargo. See بهرت (3). 1. A store; stock; heap ( ḍher ); accumulation. 2. Filling in or up; stuffing; padding; stuff. Bhartī ke sher haiṅ . One or two good lines set in a vast deal of stuffing. (Said of bad verses). 3. Admission; enlistment; enrolment; recruiting. 4. Invention; fabrication; fib; falsehood. bhartī-shudā , Ped. Enlisted; enrolled; registered. bhartī kar'nā , v. a. 1. To fill; load; lade. 2. To store up; stack; heap; lay up; accumulate. Sagro kamāī sautinyā ke ghar bhartī kar delan . Mag. All his earnings go to the co-wife. 3. To raise, levy (soldiers, etc); engage; enlist; enroll; enter on a register; recruit; hire or employ (a servant). 4. To invent; fabricate; romance. Aisī bhartī kartā hai ki sach mālūm hotā hai. bhartī honā , v. n. To be enlisted, etc. khogīr kī bhartī , n. f. A padding of rags, etc.; mere stuffing.
s. f. 1. Completion. 2. Filling. 3. Loading, lading, cargo, burden (of a ship, &c.). 4. (vulgarly used for) Promotion. بهرتي کرنا bhartī karnā, To recruit, to load.
subs: v.t. To fill, load, lade; to store up, lay up, accumulate; to admit, enter (in a register), enrol, recruit, enlist; to engage or hire (a servant); to invent, fabricate
v.n. To be admitted, be enrolled, be enlisted.

def: s.m. A worker in the metal called bhart, a brazier.
more: n. m. A cooking pot made of bharat metal. Mūl bharatye ke maiṅ neṛe āj, balam, nā jāūṅgī, Sāsaṛ dāl pakāwe āchhī, vā sūṅ hī pakw/?/ ūṅgī . W. Rus. Song. The cooking-pot, my spouse, I'll not go near today, 'Your Ma cooks dāl so well!' so she shall cook, I say!
s. m. A brasier, a worker in the metal called بهرت bhart.

def: s.m. (in comp.) = bhartā, 'Cherisher,' &c.
s.f. Upbraiding, reviling, cursing; reproach, curse, abuse; threat, menace.
s.m. Pimp; cuckold, &c. = bhaṛu,ā, q.u.
s.m. Name of Śiva; n. of Vishṇu; husband, lord; gold.

s.m. One who lives on the earnings of a prostitute; pimp, procurer, panderer; attendant on a dancing girl (who beats the mridang, and assists her in the chorus when she is singing); blackguard; fool, blockhead.
more: s. m. A pimp, one who lives on what a prostitute earns.

def: (from بهڙوا) s. f. 1. Pandarism, pimping. 2. The wages of prostitution.
s.f. Panderism, pimping; earnings of a pimp.
s.m. A small species of jackal.
s.m. Fried meat.

n. m. H. bhāṛ pimping. A pimp; procurer; pander. 1. Jo maih kasbī huī, aur tum khasam kahlāte ho mere, Agar bhaṛwe na ṭhaire tum, to phir batlāo kyā ṭhaire? Wom. Rāhat. If I'm a strumpet while you are my husband known, Say what are you to me if not a pimp self-shown? 2. Ranḍī kis kī jorū, aur bhaṛvā kis kā sālā? Prov. Whose wife is the harlot, and who is brother-in-law to the pimp? (They can never be your own). holī kā bhaṛwā , n. m. One who personates the fool at the Hindu carnival called Holī.
s.f. = bharāʼī, q.v. s.f. Filling, stuffing ( = bharāwaṭ); — price paid for filling or stuffing.
more: n. f. 1. Bawdry; pimping. 2. The earnings of a pimp.

def: v.n. To pander, procure, pimp.
v.t. To cause to be filled, &c. = bharānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to be full; to cause to fill; to fill; to feed its young (a bird); to have (a mare) covered.
more: v. a. Caus. of بهرنا q. v.
v. a. Caus. of بهرَنا q. v. To pander; procure; pimp ( milvānā ).

def: s.f. Wife, spouse; married woman; a musical mode; the wife of a rāg or deity presiding over a musical mode
more: n. f. A wife; a married woman.
s. f. A wife, married woman; a musical mode, the wife of a rāg or deity presiding over a musical mode.
subs: s.m. A husband under the rule of his wife, a hen-pecked husband.

def: adj. & s.m. To be borne; to be supported, to be cherished or nourished, to be maintained; dependent for a livelihood on another; — servant, dependent, mercenary.
s.m. Wifehood, state of being a wife; condition of a wife.
s.m. Ashes ( = bhasma, bhasam, q.v.)
s.f. Pudendum muliebre; mons veneris; the sun; a mouth.
s.m. = bhasakkū, q.v. s.f. One who eats much, or often; stuffer, crammer, gormandizer.
s.f. One who eats much, or often; stuffer, crammer, gormandizer.
more: n. f. bhasakkū, bhasakkaṛ , n. m. A woman who eats often or much.

def: v.t. To chew, to eat, eat up, devour, consume (see bhakshnā).
more: v. n. S. भक्ष् to eat. To fall upon food; gormandize; tuck in; devour. Bahū ke bhasakne ko āne roz ke pān chāhiyeṅ .

def: v.n. To fall, drop down.
v.t. To cause to float, to set afloat, to launch.
more: E. v. a. S. वह् to flow. To launch. Caus. of بهسنا q. v.
(causal of بهسنا) v. a. To launch, to set afloat.

def: adj. Loose, flabby, flaccid; soft; weak ( = phasphasā; pilpilā).
adj. Flabby, loose (as meat).
s.f. Language, speech; the vernacular of any country; dialect; the Hindī language; a word; plaint at law; an epithet of Sarasvatī (the goddess of speech); one of the rāginīs or musical modes
subs: bhāshātmik (bhāshā + ātmaka) = bhāshā-bandh, adj. Written in a vernacular.

def: s.m. Speaking, talking, saying; speech, talk; language.
ex: اَپْنا یِہ بھاشَن کِسی اَور کو دینا۔
apnā ye bhāśan kisī aur ko denā.
Give this lecture to someone else.

def: part. & s.m. Spoken, uttered, said; — speech, utterance.
s.m. Speaking, talking; any work in the common or vernacular speech; explanatory work, exposition, commentary.
s.f. A species of beet, Beta Bengalensis.
adj. & s.m. Light-causing, shining, glittering, resplendent; — the sun; fire; gold; a hero; name of a famous Hindū astronomer (commonly called Bhāskarāćārya); the plant Calotropis gigantea.
more: s. m. 1. The sun. 2. Name of a Hindū astronomer, author of the Siddhānta Shiromani and other works.

def: s.f. Ashes, cinders
more: n. m. bhasmī , n. f. S. भसम from भस् to consume. Ashes. bhasam asnān . The act of smearing the body with the ashes of cow-dung. bhasam-patrī , n. f. A cant term for gāṅjhā the hemp plant. Lāo to bhasam patrī, Na bache bāman, na bache chhatrī . Bring now the hempen liquor strong, Brahman nor Kshatrī shall resist it long. bhasam tikkā , n. m. Burnt flesh or meat. bhasam ramānā , v. a. To rub ashes on the body. Sakhī rī, nahīṅ chandan, nahīṅ ketkī, nahīṅ phūl sugandh, Yeh bhauṅrā Brij-bāulā, bhasam ramāwe aṅg . Dohā. My dear, not sandal wood, nor ketkī , nor sweet-scented flower, This beetle mad rolls in the ashes of his love each hour. bhasam kar'nā , v. a. To turn or reduce to ashes; calcine; burn. Yār ko karūṅ pyār, khasam ko karūṅ bhasam, laṛke ko karūṅ chaṭnī . Prov. My gallant I'll love, my spouse to ashes burn, my children all to pickle turn. bhasam honā , v. n. 1. To be reduced to a…
subs: (S. bhasmasnān), s.m. The act of smearing the body with the ashes of cow-dung
s.m. A certain demon who had the power to reduce to ashes any one on whose head he placed his hand
s.f. The hemp plant; gāṅjhā (a cant term)
s.m. Meat burnt or roasted on cinders, chop, &c. = bhas-tikkā, q.v.
v.n. To rub ashes on the body
v.t. To reduce to ashes; to burn, calcine
s.f. The sissoo-tree, Dalbergia sissoo, or a variety of it
v.n. To be reduced to ashes; to be digested, consumed, utterly destroyed, &c.; to be overcome with rage, be in a great rage.

def: s.m. Morbid appetite with general decay; disease of the eyes, indistinctness of vision (arising from a thickening of the membranes)
s.m. What is reduced to ashes; ashes; anything utterly consumed or destroyed; any perishing thing.
s.f. Ashes; ashes of a corpse; — place of cremation
more: n. f. 1. The ashes of the corpse. 2. The place of cremation.
subs: s.m. The act of reducing to ashes; completely consuming or burning; calcining
part. Reduced to ashes calcined (as a metal).

def: v.n. To be manifest, to appear, be known.
more: v. n. To be known, to appear.

def: v.n. To float, float away, be carried down the stream; to go down, sink; to be drowned. (In some of its meanings the word would seem to point to the S. rt. vah, as its source).
more: v. n. 1. To go down the stream. 2. To sink; fall or go down; to be drowned. 1. Bālū kī bhīt bhas gaī . 2. Ūṅṭ, ghoṛā bhas gaël, gadhā pūchhe kitnā pānī ? E. Prov. Horse and camel drowned, the donkey asks, is it deep?

def: s. m. A steak, chop, collop.
s.f. Bellows ( = bhaṭhī).
s.m. Husband's eldest brother, brother-in-law.
more: s. m. A husbandʼs elder brother.

def: adj. & s.m. Shining, splendid; — crystal; a hero; Costus speciosus or Arabicus.
v.t. = bhasānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to float, to set afloat, to launch.
adj. & s.m. Shining, radiant, &c. ( = bhāsur); — the sun, daylight; day; — Costus speciosus or Arabicus.

s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest, &c. = bhātā and bhattā, qq.v.); — boiled rice; food; — a marriage ceremony which consists in the mother's family presenting rice, mūṅg, garments, money, &c. to the bride (generally on the second day's feast); — name of a soil to the north of the Ganges (that retains its humidity for a long time, and contains a large quantity of nitre); uneven ground
more: n. m. S. भक्तम, Pr. bhatto . 1. Boiled rice; food. Bhūkā Baṅgālī 'bhāt, bhāt' pukāre . Prov. The hungry Bengālī cries out, 'rice, rice.' 2. A ceremony in which rice, mūṅg , garments, money, etc. are presented to the bride or bridegroom by the maternal grandfather. mātā kā bhāt . An offering of boiled rice made to a goddess.
n. m. bhāṭan , n. f. S. भट्ट from भट to speak. 1. A bard; minstrel; troubadour; a chronicler of ancient days; genealogist. 2. Met . A panegyrist; eulogist; flatterer.
subs: s.m. The invitation to the grand-parents of the bride to attend the ceremony of bhāt and to make the usual presents (generally given by the bride's mother).

def: s.m. Dawn, early morning.
s.m. Name of a particular mixed caste of hereditary panegyrists; bard, minstrel, panegyrist, genealogist or family bard; eulogist; flatterer.
more: s. m. Name of a tribe, a bard. See بندي and ماگده.

def: s.f. Fire-place, oven, furnace, kiln ( = bhaṭṭhā); pit; hole (especially of an animal, as a fox, &c.), cave, lair, den; — misfortune; curse
more: s. f. 1. An oven, a furnace, a kiln. 2. Curse, misfortune. بهٿ پڙنا bhaṭ-paṛnā, v. n. To be cursed, to be unfortunate, to be lost ( lit. to fall into the fire).
s. m. A warriour, a hero.
subs: To fall into misfortune, be unfortunate, be ruined or lost, to be cursed
intj. (colloq.) Go to blazes!; curse you!; hang it! rot it! (an exclamation of anger or disgust).

def: s.m. Warrior, hero; soldier, combatant; outcast of a particular tribe; barbarian.
s.m. A kind of grass.
s. f. The name of a prickly plant. (Solanum jacquini, Willd. ).
s. f. The name of a prickly plant. (Solanum jacquini, Willd. ).

s.m. = bhattā, q.v. s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest; ploughman's wages in kind; additional allowance, extra allowance or pay to public servants on special duty, or to officers and soldiers (commonly called 'batta'); subsistence money; travelling allowance
more: n. m. H. baḍhnā , to increase. 1. A current; tide (A. mad ); ebb-tide. 2. A stone. 3. An egg plant. juār bhāṭā ; A jazr o mad , n. m. Flood tide.
adj. Agreeable; pleasing; beloved; dear. Khāīye man-bhātā, pahnye jag-bhātā . Prov. Eat what you please, dress for the world.
s. m. An extra allowance to troops on service. See بهته.

def: s.m. = bhāṭhā, q.v. s.f. Quiver (for arrows).

s.m. The egg-plant = bhanṭā, q.v. s.m. = bhattā, q.v. s.m. def: s.f. The colocynth or bitter apple.
adj. Agreeable to the mind, pleasing, charming ( = bhāyā and man-bhāwan).
s.m. One who presents bhāt; maker of presents at the ceremony of bhāt; a relative of the bride or bridegroom on the mother's side; a term of abuse.
s.f. The avocation of a bhāṭ or bard; the encomiums of a bard, praise, panegyric, eulogy; adulation, flattery; — begging, mendicancy.
more: s. f. Praise, panegyrick, the encomiums of a بهاٿ bhāṭ, which see.

def: n. f. H. bhāṭ a bard. 1. The avocation of a bard. 2. Praise; panegyric; adulation. 3. Honeyed words; incense; wheedling; flattery. 4. Begging; mendicancy; begging from door to door. Kamaulā se hāth, goṛ thaklaīn, to bhaṭaī kare laglan . Bhoj. With labouring tired his arms and legs, to begging alms given o'er.

v. n. H. bāṭ road, khonā to lose. 1. To go astray; wander; miss the right path; lose one's way; take the wrong way. Woh kāṅṭā jo dil meṅ khaṭaktā rahā, To sahra meṅ yeh bhī bhaṭaktā rahā . Mīr Hasan. 2. To wander after; to haunt (the spirit); long or pine for. Bachche ke līye jī bhaṭaktā hai . 3. To deviate; err; depart from the right.
v.n. To go astray, to stray, wander, miss the right path, lose the path or way; to err, to deviate or depart from a right course; to be restless or disquieted, to long or pine (for, ke liye).
more: v. n. To go astray, to stray, wander, miss the right path, lose (the way).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Wandering, straying, astray
subs: v.n. To go wandering about; to wander about being astray.

def: adj. Wandering; astray. bhūlā bhaṭkā , n. m. A straggler; one who has lost his way.
v.t. = bhaṭkānā, q.v. v.t. To cause to stray or wander, to lead astray; to mislead, bewilder, baulk, deceive; to scare.
s.f. A female of the bhāṭ caste; wife of a bhāṭ; female minstrel.
more: s. f. The wife of a بهاٿ bhāṭ.

def: s.m. Husband
more: W., n. m. S. भर्त्ता A husband ( khāvind ). Jawān jāë patāl, buṛhyā māṅge bhatār . Prov. Young maidens die, old crones for husbands try.

def: s.m. Name of a subdivision of the Gaur rājpūts.
s.f. Quiver (for arrows).
s.m. Flow, course; stream, current; tide, ebb-tide (see bhāṭhī).
more: s. m. A current or stream, the ebbtide.

def: s.f. Bellows = bhāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. Current, stream; down-stream; — adj. With the current ( = bhaṭhiyāl. — The word, in its Prakrit form, occurs in Sanskrit, in the compound rāya-bhāṭī, 'royal, or main, stream').
more: s. f. Down the river, with the current.

def: s.f. Bellows.
n. f. Down the river; with the current.
adj. = bhaṭhiyāl, q.v.
more: adj. Down, below, down the river, with the stream. See بهٿهيال.

def: s.m. A kind of mars̤iya or elegy sung in praise of the brothers Hasan and Husain.
adj. With the current or stream, down the river and not with the flood tide.
more: 1. adj. With the current, i. e. down the river and not with the flood tide. 2. s. m. A kind of مرثيه or elegiack verses sung in praise of Hasan and Husain.

def: v.n. To go down the river, or down stream; to ebb (the tide); — v.t. To cause to go down or sink; to ruin (a person).
more: v. n. To go down the river; to ebb (the tide).

def: s. m. A sutler, an innkeeper, one who prepares victuals for travellers in a سرا sarā.
s.m. One who keeps a sarāʼe, one who prepares victuals for travellers in a sarāʼe; innkeeper; sutler; baker.
s.f. Woman who carries on the business of an inn-keeper; wife of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.f. Woman who carries on the business of an inn-keeper; wife of a bhaṭhiyārā.
s.m. Sarāʼe, caravansary, inn, eating-house; — low place.
more: s. m. An inn, a caravansary, an eatinghouse.

def: s.m. The business, or the status, of a bhaṭhiyārā.
more: s. m. The business of a بهٿهيارا bhaṭhiyārā.

def: v. n. 1. To go down the river; to ebb (the tide). 2. To cause to sink or go down, ruin one.

s.f. = bhāṅti, q.v. s.f. Way, manner, mode, method, style, fashion; variety, kind, sort, class; — bhāṅṭ-bhāṅṭ or bhāṅti-bhāṅti (-ke), adj. Various kinds (of), various, diverse, of many kinds or sorts, miscellaneous, multifarious
subs: bhalī-bhāṅt, adv. Well, thoroughly, fully, quite, soundly, severely, &c.
kisī bhāṅti adv. By some means

def: s.f. Down-stream, &c. = bhāṭhī, q.v.
s.f. A Mohammadan ceremony which consists in sending cooked rice or other food, for three days, to the house in which a death has taken place; the food, &c. so sent.
more: n. m. One who presents bhāt (2); a relative of the bride or bridegroom on the mothers' side; an abuse.

def: s.m. Brother's son, nephew; wife's brother's son.
more: n. m. S. भातृज Brother's son; nephew.

def: s.m. Name of a class of inferior Rājpūts.

s.f The prickly plant Solanum Jacquini (used medicinally for diarrhœa and coughs: — syn. bang-kaṭīyā; ban-kaṭaiyā).

subs: (bhaṭṭā+āćārya), s.m. A great doctor or philosopher; a title given to a learned Brāhman, or to any great teacher or celebrated instructor.

def: s.m. Cooked rice = bhāt, q.v. s.m. Advances to ploughmen without interest, &c. = bhātā and bhattā, qq.v.); — boiled rice; food; — a marriage ceremony which consists in the mother's family presenting rice, mūṅg, garments, money, &c. to the bride (generally on the second day's feast); — name of a soil to the north of the Ganges (that retains its humidity for a long time, and contains a large quantity of nitre); uneven ground
subs: s.m. The invitation to the grand-parents of the bride to attend the ceremony of bhāt and to make the usual presents (generally given by the bride's mother).

more: Mah. Wom. n. f. H. bhāt boiled rice. A Mahomedan ceremony which consists in giving food, etc. for three days to the members of the family of the deceased by his nearest relations. Ham ko pīṭe, hamārī bhattī khāë, Dil meṅ gar kuchh malāl īskā lāë . Zahr-i-ishq. Shauq. May I be dead, and eaten the cakes funereal, If in your mind there be of grief a trace at all.

n. f. S. भ्राष्ट्र. 1. A furnace; kiln; an alembic. Kuchh paktā hai, kuchh bhuntā hai, pakvān, miṭhāī, piṭṭhī hai, Jab dekhā khūb to ākhir ko nā chūlhā, bhāṛ, na bhaṭṭī hai . Nazīr. But now 'twas boiling, frying brisk puffs, cakes, and sweets, and all, Look close, and lo! nor fireplace, hearth, nor oven there at all. 2. A distillery; a distiller's fireplace. 3. A washerman's cauldron for boiling clothes. bhaṭṭī chaṛhā'nā , v. a. To boil clothes for cleansing. bhaṭṭī-dār , n. m. A distiller; one who keeps a spirit-shop; one who sells and manufactures spirituous liquors.
